title: Resolve MeshCore command drift across firmware and clients
description: Why this exists: project-LICHEN-9c4y.1 research found source drift between MeshCore firmware, docs, Flutter client, and Python client. Firmware e8d3c53 returns OK for CMD_SEND_CHANNEL_TXT_MSG while docs/Flutter imply MSG_SENT in some paths, and meshcore_py 5bac357 lacks newer firmware command enums such as CMD_SEND_CHANNEL_DATA=0x3e and CMD_SEND_RAW_PACKET=0x41. What needs to be done: build a pinned command/response matrix against the target MeshCore app/client versions, decide which response behavior LICHEN should emulate for compatibility, and add tests/vectors for divergent cases. Acceptance criteria: drift table records source SHAs and file refs; selected LICHEN behavior is justified; native_sim/host tests cover SEND_CHANNEL_TXT_MSG ack behavior and unsupported/stale client commands.
notes: Resolution evidence 2026-07-01: command drift is resolved for current LICHEN MVP behavior by project-LICHEN-9c4y.18 vectors and meshcore_adapter native_sim coverage. Source baselines are pinned in test/vectors/meshcore_app_compat.json: MeshCore firmware e8d3c53ba1ea863937081cd0caad759b832f3028, meshcore-open Flutter e1b8d6ec97d6ccca0eee8122864092f3af3f2062, meshcore_py 5bac3573b51c4298062881885b6d15a994109076, meshcore-cli 3ad12c07beaac21210ed9b4b04c1fe8438722ecb, meshcore.js 306dadac1cdacfd071ab6899e5c84e7def7c4425. Decision: LICHEN MVP does not emulate upstream firmware OK or app/doc MSG_SENT/SENT behavior for CMD_SEND_CHANNEL_TXT_MSG yet; it returns RESP_CODE_ERR + ERR_CODE_UNSUPPORTED_CMD (0101) until project-LICHEN-9c4y.7.2 / local message ingress and policy are implemented, because ACKing would imply a LICHEN message was accepted for mesh transmission. Vectors record this as send_channel_txt_msg_ack_drift with drift_note. Direct SEND_TXT_MSG also returns 0101 until public identity/peer projection exists. Stale/newer client command drift is covered by vectors for meshcore_py-missing/newer firmware commands: 0x3c GET_ALLOWED_REPEAT_FREQ -> 0101, 0x3e SEND_CHANNEL_DATA -> 0101, 0x3f SET_DEFAULT_FLOOD_SCOPE -> 0101, 0x40 GET_DEFAULT_FLOOD_SCOPE -> supported null response 1c, 0x41 SEND_RAW_PACKET -> 0101. Out-of-range/unknown stale commands 0x00, 0x42, 0xff return 0101. Self-advert 0x07 returns 0101, not OK/no-op. Tests/evidence from project-LICHEN-9c4y.18: local Python vectors PASS 74/74; EC2 native_sim meshcore_adapter PASS 15/15; EC2 Twister meshcore_adapter PASS 1/1 scenario, 15 cases, 0 warnings. The generated Zephyr fixture consumes the deterministic adapter subset from meshcore_app_compat.json, including SEND_CHANNEL_TXT_MSG drift and newer/stale unsupported command cases.
close_reason: Completed: command drift behavior is decided, justified, pinned to MeshCore source baselines, covered by meshcore_app_compat vectors, and consumed by native_sim meshcore_adapter generated-vector tests. SEND_CHANNEL_TXT_MSG returns ERR_UNSUPPORTED until LICHEN local message ingress exists; newer/stale/unknown command behavior is deterministic.
